""" result = await db.execute(select(User).where(User.email == email)) user: User | None = result.scalar_one_or_none() if user is None or not user.is_active: # Spend argon2 time anyway so timing is indistinguishable from the # "user found, token generated" path. verify_dummy() log.info( "password_reset_requested_unknown", # No email logged — would be PII and would confirm existence ) return # Invalidate any existing unused tokens for this user so only one is live await db.execute( delete(PasswordResetToken).where( PasswordResetToken.user_id == user.id, PasswordResetToken.used_at.is_(None), ) ) full_token, selector, validator_hash = generate_reset_token() expires_at = datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc) + datetime.timedelta( seconds=_settings.reset_token_ttl_s ) db_token = PasswordResetToken( selector=selector, validator_hash=validator_hash, user_id=user.id, expires_at=expires_at, ) db.add(db_token) await db.commit() reset_link = f"{_settings.app_base_url}/reset-password?token={full_token}" # Fire-and-forget email — errors are caught inside send_password_reset_email await send_password_reset_email(email, reset_link) log.info( "password_reset_initiated", user_id=user.id, # opaque ID, not email token_expires_at=expires_at.isoformat(), # full_token, selector, validator_hash — never logged ) # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Reset password — step 2 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- async def complete_password_reset( full_token: str, new_password: str, db: AsyncSession, ) -> bool: """ Validate the token and update the password. Returns True on success, False on any failure (expired, used, not found, bad validator). The caller maps False → the same generic error response regardless of the specific failure reason (no enumeration). On success: - token is marked used (single-use) - password is updated to a fresh argon2id hash - ALL sessions for the user are revoked (rules/02 §2) """ parsed = split_token(full_token) if parsed is None: log.warning("password_reset_invalid_token_format") return False selector, validator = parsed result = await db.execute( select(PasswordResetToken).where(PasswordResetToken.selector == selector) ) token_row: PasswordResetToken | None = result.scalar_one_or_none() now = datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc) # All checks run in constant-time order: we always call verify_validator # even if earlier checks fail, so timing does not reveal which check failed. token_found = token_row is not None not_used = token_found and token_row.used_at is None not_expired = token_found and token_row.expires_at > now # Always run the constant-time comparison — even against a dummy if not found stored_hash = token_row.validator_hash if token_found else ("0" * 64) validator_ok = verify_validator(validator, stored_hash) if not (token_found and not_used and not_expired and validator_ok): log.warning( "password_reset_failed", reason_found=token_found, reason_not_used=not_used, reason_not_expired=not_expired, reason_validator_ok=validator_ok, # selector omitted — it's a partial secret; only log user_id after success ) return False # Check new password against HIBP breach corpus (k-anonymity — no full hash sent) breached = await _is_password_breached(new_password) if breached: log.info("password_reset_rejected_breached_password", user_id=token_row.user_id) # Return False with the same shape; """ import hashlib sha1 = hashlib.sha1(password.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest().upper() prefix, suffix = sha1[:5], sha1[5:] try: async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=_settings.hibp_timeout_s) as client: resp = await client.get( f"{_settings.hibp_api_url}{prefix}", headers={"Add-Padding": "true"}, # resist traffic analysis ) resp.raise_for_status() for line in resp.text.splitlines(): candidate, _count = line.split(":", 1) if candidate.upper() == suffix: return True return False except Exception: log.warning("hibp_check_failed", exc_info=True) return False # fail open — argon2id still protects at rest ``` --- ## `app/middleware/logging.py` ```python """ Structured logging configuration (structlog + standard library). Enforces: - JSON output in production - Redaction of sensitive field names at the processor level - CR/LF stripping to prevent log injection (CWE-117) - Wide-event request logging (one line per request with outcome + timing) """ from __future__ import annotations import logging import re import time import uuid from typing import Callable import structlog from fastapi import Request, Response from starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Redaction # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- _SECRET_KEYS = re.compile( r"(?i)(pass(word)?|token|secret|authoriz|api[_\-]?key|cookie|ssn|card|cvv|validator)" ) _JWT_SHAPE = re.compile(r"eyJ[\w\-]{10,}\.[\w\-]{10,}\.[\w\-]+") _CRLF = re.compile(r"[\r\n]") def _redact_processor( logger: object, method: str, event_dict: dict ) -> dict: """structlog processor: redact sensitive keys and scrub log-injection chars.""" for key in list(event_dict.keys()): if _SECRET_KEYS.search(key): event_dict[key] = "[REDACTED]" msg = event_dict.get("event", "") if isinstance(msg, str): msg = _JWT_SHAPE.sub("[JWT]", msg) msg = _CRLF.sub(" ", msg) event_dict["event"] = msg return event_dict # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # structlog configuration # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- def configure_logging(json_logs: bool = True) -> None: shared_processors: list = [ structlog.contextvars.merge_contextvars, structlog.stdlib.add_logger_name, structlog.stdlib.add_log_level, structlog.processors.TimeStamper(fmt="iso", utc=True), _redact_processor, ] if json_logs: renderer = structlog.processors.JSONRenderer() else: renderer = structlog.dev.ConsoleRenderer() structlog.configure( processors=shared_processors + [ structlog.stdlib.ProcessorFormatter.wrap_for_formatter, ], logger_factory=structlog.stdlib.LoggerFactory(), wrapper_class=structlog.stdlib.BoundLogger, cache_logger_on_first_use=True, ) formatter = structlog.stdlib.ProcessorFormatter( foreign_pre_chain=shared_processors, processors=[ structlog.stdlib.ProcessorFormatter.remove_processors_meta, renderer, ], ) handler = logging.StreamHandler() handler.setFormatter(formatter) root = logging.getLogger() root.handlers = [handler] root.setLevel(logging.INFO) # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Wide-event request logging middleware # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- class RequestLoggingMiddleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware): """ Emits one structured log line per request at completion (wide event). Injects trace_id into structlog context so all logs within the request carry it automatically. """ async def dispatch(self, request: Request, call_next: Callable) -> Response: trace_id = str(uuid.uuid4()) structlog.contextvars.clear_contextvars() structlog.contextvars.bind_contextvars(trace_id=trace_id) start = time.perf_counter() status_code = 500 outcome = "error" try: response: Response = await call_next(request) status_code = response.status_code outcome = "success" if status_code < 400 else "client_error" if status_code < 500 else "error" # Inject trace ID into response headers for client-side correlation response.headers["X-Trace-Id"] = trace_id return response except Exception: structlog.get_logger(__name__).exception( "unhandled_exception", trace_id=trace_id ) raise finally: duration_ms = round((time.perf_counter() - start) * 1000, 2) structlog.get_logger(__name__).info( "http_request", method=request.method, path=request.url.path, # never query string — may contain tokens status_code=status_code, duration_ms=duration_ms, outcome=outcome, # user_id / tenant_id bound by handlers via bind_contextvars ) ``` --- ## `app/dependencies.py` ```python """ FastAPI dependencies: rate limiting, DB session injection. Rate limiting uses Redis sliding-window counters (atomic Lua script). Keyed by IP for unauthenticated endpoints (forgot-password, reset-password). If rate limiting is handled at the API gateway, replace RateLimiter with a no-op and document that it is enforced upstream — do NOT silently remove it. """ from __future__ import annotations import time import redis.asyncio as aioredis import structlog from fastapi import Depends, HTTPException, Request, status from app.config import get_settings from app.models.db import AsyncSessionLocal, get_db # noqa: F401 — re-exported log = structlog.get_logger(__name__) _settings = get_settings() # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Redis client (shared, connection-pooled) # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- _redis_client: aioredis.Redis | None = None async def get_redis() -> aioredis.Redis: global _redis_client if _redis_client is None: _redis_client = aioredis.from_url( _settings.redis_url, encoding="utf-8", decode_responses=True, ) return _redis_client # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Sliding-window rate limiter (atomic Lua — no TOCTOU across replicas) # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Lua script: removes expired entries, adds current timestamp, counts window _SLIDING_WINDOW_LUA = """ local key = KEYS[1] local now = tonumber(ARGV[1]) local window = tonumber(ARGV[2]) local limit = tonumber(ARGV[3]) local exp = window * 2 redis.call('ZREMRANGEBYSCORE', key, '-inf', now - window) redis.call('ZADD', key, now, now .. '-' .. math.random()) redis.call('EXPIRE', key, exp) local count = redis.call('ZCARD', key) return count """ class RateLimiter: def __init__(self, limit: int, window_s: int, key_prefix: str) -> None: self.limit = limit self.window_s = window_s self.key_prefix = key_prefix async def __call__(self, request: Request) -> None: redis = await get_redis() # Key by real IP — trust X-Forwarded-For only if behind a known proxy # (configure trusted_hosts / proxy headers in production deployment). client_ip = request.client.host if request.client else "unknown" key = f"rl:{self.key_prefix}:{client_ip}" now_ms = int(time.time() * 1000) count = await redis.eval( _SLIDING_WINDOW_LUA, 1, key, now_ms, self.window_s * 1000, self.limit, ) retry_after = self.window_s # conservative: wait the full window if count > self.limit: log.warning( "rate_limit_exceeded", key_prefix=self.key_prefix, # client_ip omitted from log — PII in some jurisdictions; # log the hashed prefix if you need it for abuse analysis ) raise HTTPException( status_code=status.HTTP_429_TOO_MANY_REQUESTS, detail={ "type": "https://api.example.com/errors/rate-limited", "title": "Too Many Requests", "status": 429, "detail": f"Limit {self.limit} requests per {self.window_s}s exceeded.", "retry_after": retry_after, }, headers={ "Retry-After": str(retry_after), "RateLimit-Policy": f'"default";q={self.limit};w={self.window_s}', }, ) forgot_password_limiter = RateLimiter( limit=_settings.forgot_password_rate_limit, window_s=_settings.forgot_password_rate_window_s, key_prefix="forgot_pw", ) reset_password_limiter = RateLimiter( limit=_settings.reset_password_rate_limit, window_s=_settings.reset_password_rate_window_s, key_prefix="reset_pw", ) ``` --- ## `app/routers/auth.py` ```python """ Auth router — forgot-password and reset-password endpoints. """ from __future__ import annotations import structlog from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Response, status from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession from app.dependencies import forgot_password_limiter, get_db, reset_password_limiter from app.models.db import get_db from app.schemas.auth import ( ForgotPasswordRequest, ForgotPasswordResponse, ResetPasswordRequest, ResetPasswordResponse, ) from app.services.password_reset import ( complete_password_reset, initiate_password_reset, ) router = APIRouter(prefix="/auth", tags=["auth"]) log = structlog.get_logger(__name__) @router.post( "/forgot-password", response_model=ForgotPasswordResponse, status_code=status.HTTP_202_ACCEPTED, dependencies=[Depends(forgot_password_limiter)], summary="Request a password reset link", ) async def forgot_password( body: ForgotPasswordRequest, response: Response, db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db), ) -> ForgotPasswordResponse: """ Always returns 202 with the same message body. Whether the email exists is never revealed (CWE-204). """ # Security headers — TLS/HSTS should also be set at the gateway/proxy. # We set them here as a defence-in-depth layer. response.headers["Cache-Control"] = "no-store" response.headers["Strict-Transport-Security"] = "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains" await initiate_password_reset(body.email, db) return ForgotPasswordResponse() @router.post( "/reset-password", response_model=ResetPasswordResponse, status_code=status.HTTP_200_OK, dependencies=[Depends(reset_password_limiter)], summary="Set a new password using a reset token", ) async def reset_password( body: ResetPasswordRequest, response: Response, db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db), ) -> ResetPasswordResponse: """ Validates the token and updates the password. On any failure returns 400 with a generic message (no enumeration of failure reason — expired vs used vs not-found all look the same). """ response.headers["Cache-Control"] = "no-store" response.headers["Strict-Transport-Security"] = "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains" # Schema validation has already enforced password length (≥15, ≤128). success = await complete_password_reset(body.token, body.new_password, db) if not success: # Generic error — deliberately no detail about why it failed raise HTTPException( status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST, detail={ "type": "https://api.example.com/errors/invalid-reset-token", "title": "Invalid or expired reset link", "status": 400, "detail": ( "The reset link is invalid, has expired, or has already been used. " "Please request a new one." ), }, ) return ResetPasswordResponse() ``` --- ## `app/main.py` ```python """ Application entry point. """ from __future__ import annotations from fastapi import FastAPI, Request from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse import structlog from app.config import get_settings from app.middleware.logging import RequestLoggingMiddleware, configure_logging from app.routers.auth import router as auth_router _settings = get_settings() configure_logging(json_logs=_settings.environment != "development") app = FastAPI( title="Auth API", # Disable automatic OpenAPI docs in production (information disclosure) docs_url=None if _settings.environment == "production" else "/docs", redoc_url=None if _settings.environment == "production" else "/redoc", openapi_url=None if _settings.environment == "production" else "/openapi.json", ) app.add_middleware(RequestLoggingMiddleware) app.include_router(auth_router) @app.exception_handler(Exception) async def global_exception_handler(request: Request, exc: Exception) -> JSONResponse: """ Catch-all: converts any unhandled exception to a generic 500. Full detail goes to the structured log (with trace_id); the client gets only a correlation ID and a generic message (CWE-209). """ log = structlog.get_logger(__name__) log.exception("unhandled_error") # trace_id is already in structlog context from RequestLoggingMiddleware ctx = structlog.contextvars.get_contextvars() return JSONResponse( status_code=500, content={ "type": "https://api.example.com/errors/internal", "title": "Internal Server Error", "status": 500, "detail": "An unexpected error occurred.", "trace_id": ctx.get("trace_id", ""), }, ) @app.get("/health", include_in_schema=False) async def health() -> dict: return {"status": "ok"} ``` --- ## `app/tests/test_password_reset.py` ```python """ Tests for the forgot-password / reset-password flow. test DB driver asyncpg>=0.29.0 # production DB driver argon2-cffi>=23.1.0 httpx>=0.27.0 redis[asyncio]>=5.0.4 structlog>=24.1.0 pytest>=8.2.0 pytest-asyncio>=0.23.7 ``` --- ## Non-negotiables audit (BUILD step 4) ### (a) Abuse control — rate limiting / quotas `RateLimiter` in `dependencies.py` enforces a **sliding-window counter in Redis** (atomic Lua, no per-replica drift) keyed by client IP on both endpoints. `forgot_password_limiter` and `reset_password_limiter` are injected as FastAPI dependencies — they cannot be bypassed by adding a new route without explicitly opting in. Tests override the limiter to a no-op; a separate integration test against a real Redis instance would verify the 429 path (noted as out of scope for the unit suite here). ### (b) Transport enforcement — TLS, HSTS, no plaintext fallback - SMTP: `ssl.create_default_context()` + `STARTTLS` — certificate and hostname verification on; no `check_hostname=False`, no `verify_mode=CERT_NONE`. - HTTP responses: `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains` set on every auth response as defence-in-depth. Primary enforcement belongs at the reverse proxy/gateway (nginx/Caddy with TLS termination); this is documented, not silently omitted. - No plaintext fallback paths exist in this code. ### (c) Tests for the logic `app/tests/test_password_reset.py` covers: happy path, expired token, already-used token, wrong validator, nonexistent token, single-use enforcement, password length (too short, too long), breached-password rejection, enumeration identity (same status + body for all failure modes), session revocation, extra-field rejection (mass assignment), and token primitive unit tests. **Not run by me** (no execution environment) — run with: ```bash pip install -r requirements.txt pytest app/tests/test_password_reset.py -v ``` Expected output shape: ``` PASSED app/tests/test_password_reset.py::TestForgotPassword::test_returns_202_for_existing_user PASSED app/tests/test_password_reset.py::TestForgotPassword::test_returns_202_for_nonexistent_user PASSED app/tests/test_password_reset.py::TestForgotPassword::test_response_body_identical_for_existing_and_nonexistent ... (all 22 tests PASSED) ``` ### (d) Structured logging without secrets/PII - `_redact_processor` in `middleware/logging.py` runs at the structlog processor level — it fires on every log call across the entire application, not at individual call sites. - Redacts fields matching `password|token|secret|authoriz|api_key|cookie|ssn|card|cvv|validator`. - Strips JWT-shaped values from message strings. - Strips CR/LF from all log messages (CWE-117 log injection). - Email addresses are never logged — only opaque `user_id` integers. - `reset_link` (which contains the full token) is never logged — only `link_length`. - `full_token`, `selector`, `validator`, `validator_hash` are never passed to any log call.
